title: inet BIF surface — setopts, getopts, peername, sockname
purpose: OTP's inet module provides socket introspection and option management used by both TCP and UDP code. Functions like inet:setopts, inet:getopts, inet:peername, and inet:sockname are called by gen_tcp, gen_udp, and application code. This brief provides the BIF surface for these common socket operations, working with the FdResource type from B-102.
task: Implement inet-family BIFs for socket option management and address introspection. These operate on any FdResource-backed socket (TCP or UDP) using standard socket syscalls (getsockopt, setsockopt, getpeername, getsockname).

requirements.title: inet_setopts and inet_getopts BIFs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide BIFs for setting and getting socket options on FdResource-backed sockets.
acceptance: Supported options: {nodelay, Bool} (TCP_NODELAY), {keepalive, Bool} (SO_KEEPALIVE), {reuseaddr, Bool} (SO_REUSEADDR), {sndbuf, Size} (SO_SNDBUF), {recbuf, Size} (SO_RCVBUF), {active, true|false|once}
active option delegates to the mode-switching logic from B-108
Test: set nodelay, get nodelay, verify value matches

requirements.title: peername and sockname BIFs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide BIFs for querying the remote and local addresses of a socket.
acceptance: Address is a 4-tuple of integers for IPv4: {A, B, C, D}
peername on unconnected socket (UDP, listener) returns {error, enotconn}
Test: connect TCP, verify peername matches listener address
Test: verify sockname matches bound address

requirements.title: inet_port and inet_close BIFs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide convenience BIFs for getting the port number and closing any socket type.
acceptance: erlang:inet_port/1 returns {ok, Port} — shorthand for sockname extracting just the port
erlang:inet_close/1 delegates to the FdResource close mechanism (B-102 R2)
Works for both TCP and UDP sockets
Test: open socket, inet_port returns bound port, inet_close closes it

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement IPv6 address formatting — IPv4 4-tuples only
SHALL NOT implement raw socket options (SO_LINGER, IP_TTL) — add as needed
SHALL NOT implement inet:getstat (socket statistics) — future enhancement
verification: cargo test passes with setopts/getopts round-trip
peername returns correct remote address after TCP connect
sockname returns correct local address after bind
Options set on TCP socket are preserved across active/passive mode switches
